chemistry.reaction_name: Photoredox-mediated conjugate addition of glycosyl radicals to acrolein
chemistry.scope_summary: The methodology was applied to acetate- and pivaloate-protected glycosyl bromides, producing C-glycoside aldehydes that serve as intermediates for further derivatization to C-glycoamino acids and C-glycolipids

optimization_table.key_findings: Entry 4 with 4 modules gave 85% yield and >97% conversion for substrate 4

figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Planned synthesis of  C -glycoconjugates from common alde- hyde intermediate. | Scheme 2.  Conversion and TOF based on vessel size.

figures.trend: 25 mL flask shows higher conversion but lower TOF compared to 5 mm NMR tube
figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Planned synthesis of  C -glycoconjugates from common alde- hyde intermediate. | Scheme 2.  Conversion and TOF based on vessel size.

figures.key_information: Photochemical flow reactor with external blue LED illumination and reflux condenser cooling

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Higher turnover frequency in photoflow reactor compared to batch reactions

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing photoflow setup with LED strips
Optimization plot comparing vessel types and TOF values

notes: Paper focuses on photoredox-mediated C-glycoside synthesis in flow. Contains detailed reactor scheme and optimization data but lacks complete batch baseline conditions for direct comparison.
